description: CISSP Exam Trap Codes - Simplified Version (Most Actionable)

NEG.the_trap: You skim over LEAST, EXCEPT, NOT, BEST and answer the opposite
NEG.the_fix: Re-read: replace 'LEAST' with 'MOST' to see if your answer still works
NEG.red_flag: You missed a negative modifier word
NEG.study_tip: Highlight LEAST/EXCEPT/NOT/MOST with a marker during practice

ABS.the_fix: Eliminate immediately. In security, 100% certainty is impossible

example.trap_answer: This will COMPLETELY PREVENT SQL injection
example.correct: This will SIGNIFICANTLY REDUCE the risk of SQL injection

ROLE.the_trap: You pick technical fix (firewall) even when stem asks 'Manager' or 'Policy'
ROLE.the_fix: Match the role: Analyst=Technical, Manager=Policy, Owner=Budget, Custodian=Implement

example.trap_answer: Install a DLP agent (IT Admin would do this)
example.correct: Approve the Data Classification Policy (Data Owner must do this)

ROLE.red_flag: You picked technical when role suggested strategic/policy
ROLE.study_tip: Read stem LAST SENTENCE first to identify the role

ORDER.the_trap: You jump to the solution without the mandatory prerequisite step
ORDER.the_fix: Recite ISC2 lifecycles: BCP=BIA first, IR=Contain first, Forensics=RAM first

example.trap_answer: Restore from the last backup (6th step)
example.correct: Activate the warm site per DR Plan (1st step of activation)

ORDER.red_flag: You got the sequence of steps wrong
ORDER.study_tip: Draw timelines for BCP/IR/SDLC/Forensics phases and memorize order

SCOPE.the_trap: You apply on-premise logic to cloud, or confuse who owns what
SCOPE.the_fix: Define the shared model: IaaS (you=OS/Apps), PaaS (you=Data/Apps), SaaS (you=Data/Access)

example.trap_answer: Reconfigure the hypervisor (Cloud Provider's job in IaaS)
example.correct: Reconfigure the guest OS firewall (Cloud Consumer's job)

SCOPE.study_tip: Memorize CSA Responsibility Matrix for IaaS/PaaS/SaaS

ALL.the_trap: You pick specific, correct answer (Firewall) when broader answer encompasses it
ALL.the_fix: Ask: Does this include the others? If yes, pick the strategic umbrella answer
ALL.red_flag: You picked partial answer when broader option existed

GOLD.the_trap: You pick complex/crypto/technical term that looks impressive but answers wrong question
GOLD.the_fix: Eliminate irrelevant domains. Question about Authentication? Ignore Encryption

example.trap_answer: Use AES-256 encryption (Confidentiality domain, not asked)

GOLD.red_flag: You picked technically correct but domain-irrelevant answer
GOLD.study_tip: Verify answer matches the question's domain (C/I/A, Auth, Crypto, etc.)

ETHIC.the_trap: You pick vigilante option (hack back) or expedient option (call cops) too quickly
ETHIC.the_fix: Apply ISC2 Canon: Protect society > Protect organization > Report authority

example.trap_answer: Trace attacker IP and block them globally (vigilante response)
example.correct: Isolate compromised system and preserve logs for chain of custody (ethical+legal)

ETHIC.red_flag: You didn't apply ISC2 Code of Ethics
ETHIC.study_tip: Memorize ISC2 Canon: Society > Organization > Authority > Self

priority_system.description: CRITICAL traps are objective rules. HIGH traps are common confusion patterns

at_the_exam: 1️⃣ Read LAST SENTENCE first (identify role + action word)
2️⃣ Highlight: LEAST, EXCEPT, NOT, BEST, MOST, FIRST
3️⃣ Eliminate answers with: Always, Never, All, Completely
4️⃣ If stem says 'Manager/Policy', eliminate technical answers
5️⃣ If multiple answers look right, pick the BROADEST/UMBRELLA
6️⃣ Verify answer matches question domain (not a 'shiny object')
7️⃣ Apply ISC2 ethics if any ethical question

concept_types.DISTINCTION: Know how it differs from similar terms
